title: SHAL topology file, shal_version 1
description: Structural validation of a SHAL topology. Semantic checks (id uniqueness, per-bus-family address grammar, driver installed, $ref resolution, transport-kind compatibility) happen at load, after schema validation.

id.description: Optional, globally unique (uniqueness enforced at load), location-independent handle.

compatible.description: vendor,device — e.g. ti,tmp102 or shal,ssh-host. Resolved via entry points; unknown compatible fails the load.

address.description: Meaning and grammar are defined by the parent bus family and validated at load (e.g. 7-bit I2C is 0x03-0x77). ${ENV_VAR} interpolation allowed; resolved values never come from the file.

ref.description: $<id> — name pointer to an existing node. Linked, not recursed into; never routed through.

routes.description: Failover, opt-in. Declared order = priority; canonical path derives from the first route.

watchdog_ms.description: No command within N ms -> driver's safe_state(). Valid only on nodes whose driver implements an actuator capability (checked at load).

verify.description: Per-bus: re-assert real state instead of trusting the selection/connection cache (shared/untrusted buses).

insecure.description: Loud opt-out of TLS for network buses. Never a default.
